creator: gspeicher@cloudshadow.com
text:
While I am technically a "Firebird developer," I am not part of the real
effort that is going behind the development of Firebird; to claim that I
was would be taking undue credit.  As a result, I'm not in a position to
estimate when the 1.0 release will be final.  At this point, Firebird 1.0
Beta2 is out.  This fact combined with the sheer volume of messages moving
through the fb-devel mailing list suggests that the final release is on
its way.  :)
     I did recently try to upgrade the FreeBSD port so that it was in line
with the current sources.  The verdict?  The boot build is broken.  The
boot build allows one to build Firebird without having a working version
of Firebird/InterBase installed, and is something that I assume should be
a favorable quality for the FreeBSD port build process.
     So unfortunately, unless we want to get into a port that downloads
and installs Firebird binaries to do the build, the port is going to have
to wait until the boot build is fixed.
     The good news is that Firebird 2 always builds from scratch, so this
nonsense will eventually go away.  The bad news is that we have to deal
with it in the meantime, and I have no idea when FB2 will be out.  :/

Any suggestions?

Geoff
